channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Pelosi has hip replacement surgery at a US military hospital in Germany after a fall
item.description: Former House Speaker Nancy Pelosi has had hip replacement surgery at a U.S. military hospital in Germany after falling while at an event in Luxembourg with other members of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Mary Clare Jalonick And Lisa Mascaro, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Nancy Pelosi announces major surgery after fall in Luxembourg
item.description: Former House Speaker Rep. Nancy Pelosi, D-Calif., underwent successful hip surgery overseas after her fall in Luxembourg, her office revealed in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New Jersey drones: State senator wants state of emergency
item.description: One politician in New Jersey is calling for a "limited" state of emergency after what he said is a lack of government transparency over the recent unexplained wave of drone sightings.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: NYC Council speaker concerned about deportation plans
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- New York City Council Speaker Adrienne Adams has concerns about Mayor Eric Adams' goal to deport migrants who commit violent crimes in the city. Mayor Adams met with incoming border czar Tom Homan on Thursday to discuss President-elect Donald Trump’s plans for mass deportations.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Nancy Pelosi hospitalized after she 'sustained an injury' from fall on official trip to Luxembourg
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Former House Speaker Nancy Pelosi has been hospitalized after she “sustained an injury” during an official engagement in Luxembourg, according to a spokesman.
item.dc:creator: MARY CLARE JALONICK and FARNOUSH AMIRI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US swaps prisoners with China, releasing 3 convicted spies
item.description: Two Chinese spies and a Chinese national charged for disseminating child pornography were part of a recent White House prison swap as Biden's presidency nears the end.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump wants to turn the clock on daylight saving time
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump wants to turn the lights out on daylight saving time. In a post on his social media site Friday, Trump said his party would try to end the practice when he returns to office.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DC food workers pledge to make Trump officials unwelcome, echoing confrontations in first term
item.description: Washington, D.C., food workers vow to disrupt service for incoming Trump administration officials, echoing past harassment of his allies during the president-elect's first term in office.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jill Biden tells military children at Marine Corps Reserve toy drive to remember that they are loved
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Less than 10 minutes was all it took for a large pile of toys donated by the White House staff to disappear as Jill Biden and children from military families sorted them into boxes as part of the annual Marine Corps Reserve Toys for Tots campaign for the less fortunate.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: WV lawmaker arrested after allegedly threatening to kill entire region's delegation over caucus beef
item.description: West Virginia Del-elect Joseph de Soto allegedly threatened to kill Eastern Panhandle lawmakers and the House Speaker after being informed they would vote him out of the caucus.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 5 things to know about Biden’s 1,500 clemencies, pardons
item.description: President Biden pardoned 39 people and commuted the sentences of nearly 1,500 others on Thursday, a sweeping move that comes after pressure from criminal justice advocates over the surprise pardon of his son Hunter Biden.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: New Jersey drone sightings: Lawmakers press for more briefings
item.description: "Law enforcement and other agencies continue to support New Jersey and investigate the reports," the FBI said.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Amazon to contribute $1 million to Trump’s inauguration fund. Meta is also donating $1M
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Amazon is planning to donate $1 million to President-elect Donald Trump's inauguration fund, a move that comes as major tech companies seek to improve their relationship with the incoming president.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mitt Romney's Senate exit may create a vacuum of vocal, conservative Trump critics
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) — With Mitt Romney set to exit the U.S. Senate, Washington will be without one of its strongest conservative critics of Donald Trump when the president retakes the White House in the new year.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NYC 'won't be a safe haven' for migrants who commit crimes, mayor says
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Mayor Eric Adams on Thursday said he and incoming Trump administration border czar Tom Homan “have the same desire” to deport migrants who commit violent crimes in New York City.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, PIX 11

item.title: RFK Jr.'s daughter-in-law met with Ratcliffe about CIA job: Washington Post
item.description: Robert F. Kennedy Jr.’s daughter-in-law took a private meeting with John Ratcliffe, President-elect Trump’s pick to run the CIA, in an apparent bid for a high-ranking position within the agency, The Washington Post reported.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Trudo, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Khanna eyes Defense Department contracting, excess property for DOGE action
item.description: "I want the U.S. to have the greatest military in the world and the resources to counter increasingly sophisticated threats from our adversaries, but we need a more sensible approach," Khanna wrote.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: Donald Trump makes impromptu visit; Secret Service holiday party canceled last minute: Source
item.description: ”Logistics had to be done, everything had to be swept, posts had to be manned, and it just wasn’t possible to have the party Wednesday,” a source said.
item.dc:creator: Paula Froelich, NewsNation

item.title: Trump is named Time's Person of the Year, will ring the New York Stock Exchange bell
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is back in his hometown of New York where he's been honored as Time magazine’s Person of the Year and will ring the opening bell at the New York Stock Exchange.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Ontario to restrict electricity exports to U.S. and bar American-made alcohol if Trump tariffs applied
item.description: Ontario may considering barring American-made alcohol in addition to restricting electricity exports to Minnesota, Michigan and New York state.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Mayor Eric Adams to discuss immigration with incoming US border czar
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – A high-stakes talk will be held on Thursday between New York City Mayor Eric Adams and President-elect Donald Trump's incoming border czar, Tom Homan.
item.dc:creator: Justin Finch, PIX 11

item.title: Two lawmakers race to expand gun laws
item.description: Concerns about gun violence in St. Pete come as State Representative Dr. Joel Rudman and State Senator Randy Fine juggle gun laws in Tallahassee amid running for Congressional districts 1 and 6.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: FBI Director Wray to resign at end of Biden administration
item.description: FBI Director Christopher Wray on Wednesday announced he would resign from the bureau at the end of the Biden administration next month, with more than two years remaining on his term in office.
item.dc:creator: Carrie Johnson and Ryan Lucas, MPR News

item.title: Kimberly Guilfoyle is the latest Trump family ally chosen for an ambassadorship
item.description: Kimberly Guilfoyle, who got engaged to Trump's son in 2020, is the latest appointee he's chosen from his family tree. There's a long and controversial history of presidents picking relatives for jobs.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman, MPR News

item.title: Mayor Jacob Frey vetoes 2025 budget approved by Minneapolis City Council
item.description: Minneapolis Mayor Jacob Frey announced Wednesday afternoon that he is vetoing the 2025 budget approved by the city council late Tuesday night. The budget had passed with enough votes to override a veto.
item.dc:creator: Cari Spencer, MPR News

item.title: Nevada adds blockchain security to prevent repeat of 'fake electors' mischief
item.description: Nevada has integrated blockchain technology into its election certification process to add even more security to its procedures, the Nevada Secretary of State's Office announced on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump return to DC looms over Kennedy Center Honors
item.description: In a night filled with political moments — including multiple nods to President Joe Biden, a Grateful Dead tribute from former Speaker Nancy Pelosi (D-Calif.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, Fox 5
